title: Element-type column matrix governs which columns each row type accepts
description: Each row type has an explicit allowed/forbidden column set. Data-type rows accept all columns. Local calculate rows forbid readonly/constraint/required. External-lookup calculate rows (bind__oc_external='clinicaldata') additionally forbid bind__oc_itemgroup and (usually) label. note rows forbid bind__oc_itemgroup, required, and constraint. begin/end group + begin/end repeat rows accept only type, name, appearance, relevant, and bind__oc_itemgroup. OC silently hides fields that violate this and emits diagnostic errors at runtime.
rationale: OC4's XLSForm parser rejects rows where the column population doesn't match the row type. Violations are silent at form upload but surface as 'cannot be defined as type = calculate and readonly' diagnostics when the form is opened — by which point the field has already been dropped from the active definition.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} (type=${field.type}) — verify allowed/forbidden columns per the OC-5a element-type matrix. Local calculate forbids readonly/constraint/required; external-lookup calculate additionally forbids bind__oc_itemgroup and usually label; note forbids bind__oc_itemgroup/required/constraint; begin/end group/repeat accept only type/name/appearance/relevant/bind__oc_itemgroup.

effect.soft: Six element-type categories — data types (text/integer/decimal/date/time/dateTime/select_one/select_multiple), local calculate, external-lookup calculate (bind__oc_external='clinicaldata'), note, begin/end group, begin/end repeat — each with an explicit allowed-and-forbidden column set. Validate every row's column population against its row type before emitting the XLSForm.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-5a during Phase B.1a. The full 6-by-N allowed-columns matrix is too rich for the current DSL — it would benefit from a 'match_type' operator that picks an effect block per field.type value. Deferred to Phase B/C DSL extension. Today's flag + soft directive carries the matrix prose to Claude and review_flags. Kind revised from `structured` to `hybrid` during B.1a translation because the 6-by-N allowed/forbidden columns matrix requires AI judgment per row type until DSL gains a `match_type` operator; soft directive carries the matrix prose.
